metadata.title: Spanda Kārikās: The Divine Creative Pulsation
metadata.author: Vasugupta

excerpts.text: Since the limited individual Self is identical with the whole universe, inasmuch as all entities arise from him, and because of the knowledge of all subjects, he has the feeling of identity with them all, hence whether in the word, object or thought, there is no state which is not Śiva.
Even an iota of the ignorance of the nature of the integral anuttara amounts to a contrary state of the mind. It is this contrary state that constitutes transmigratory existence (saṃsāra).
